🇷🇺 A suspect convicted in the Crocus attack has died by suicide in a Moscow detention center, according to prison authorities. The inmate attempted suicide overnight at Matrosskaya Tishina. Staff tried to resuscitate him for several hours but failed. The incident was discovered via surveillance cameras. His identity has not been disclosed. An investigation is underway. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Reuters: Iran and the U.S. have been presented with a proposed framework to end the war, featuring a 2 stage deal. It begins with an immediate ceasefire, followed by negotiations toward a broader final agreement known as the “Islamabad Accord.” - Open Source Intel tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇬🇷 The Israeli and Greek defence ministries have signed a major export deal for the supply of PULS rocket artillery systems to the Greek army, worth approximately 2.3 billion shekels (about 650 million euros). The system, manufactured by Elbit Systems, includes launchers and a comprehensive package of guided rockets. The agreement includes industrial cooperation, with production of some components in Greece. Implementation will take place over four years, followed by a ten‑year support period. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Hezbollah claimed multiple attacks on Monday, including rocket barrages on Nahariya, Kiryat Shmona, Metulla and Ma'alot Tarshiha, as well as strikes on Israeli military gatherings at the Fatima Gate crossing, Ibl al-Qam, and several locations in Ainata (hills of Sedr, Ghamatha and Freiz). The group also claimed a direct hit on a Merkava tank in Rashaf and said it targeted the Givat Olga base, which belongs to the IDF Northern Command, 75 kilometres from the border, using a specialised missile and attack drones. Additionally, Hezbollah said it shot down an Israeli Hermes 450 drone over Ainata with a surface‑to‑air missile. @wfwitness

Yemen’s Houthis say they will honor a ceasefire with the United States as long as Washington stops attacks against them. A senior Houthi official, Mohammed Ali al-Houthi, said the group has no plans to target Red Sea shipping or US assets unless the conflict escalates. He added they will not attack other Muslim countries unless provoked. However, they warned that if tensions rise, they could close the strategic Bab al-Mandab Strait. They also said Saudi Red Sea ports will not be targeted as long as Saudi Arabia avoids involvement in escalation. Source: CNN
🇾🇪 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 Smoke billows over Eilat, southern occupied Palestine, after strike by Yemeni armed forces.

Open Source Intel The IDF says several drones were launched from Yemen toward southern Israel, setting off sirens in Eilat and nearby towns. Some drones were intercepted, while others lost contact and may have crashed. No injuries or damage reported. tweet

Faytuks News A clear escalation today in the targets being attacked in Iran by the Israelis/US. Another petrochemical facility in Iran was hit by airstrikes, Iranian media reports. "A few minutes ago, the Marvdasht Petrochemical Complex was targeted by an American-Zionist enemy attack," the Fars news agency reports. Fars says a fire that sparked at the complex was brought under control, and no causalities were caused. Earlier, Israel confirmed striking Iran's largest petrochemical facility in Asaluyeh. Over the weekend, Israel also struck petrochemical facilities in southern Iran. - Emanuel (Mannie) Fabian tweet

Now the two facilities, which together are responsible for about 85% of Iran's petrochemical exports - have been taken out of use and are not functioning" Asaluyeh is home to the Pars oil company which refines the oil and natural gas extracted from the South Pars oil & gas field shared with Qatar. tweet

Visioner 🇮🇱 🎯 🇮🇷 This morning, as a result of airstrikes carried out by the Israeli Air Force in Tehran, Asghar Baqeri, a member of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) of Iran, who is also known as Yazdan Miri — the commander of the 840th unit of the "Quds Force," responsible for global terrorist acts, was eliminated. tweet

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : ❗️ ❗️ 🇺🇦 🇷🇺 BIG | According to Financial Times, Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil facilities have caused nearly $1 billion in damage. ➡️ Attacks on the Primorsk and Ust-Luga terminals, which handle over 40% of Russia’s seaborne oil exports, resulted in losses of around $970 million. 🛢️ Approximately $200 million worth of oil was burned in Primorsk. ➡️ Partial capacity could be restored within weeks, but full repairs may take months. ➡️ Lukoil’s refinery in the Nizhny Novgorod region was also hit. ➡️ Russia is unable to fully protect its energy infrastructure, a reality reportedly acknowledged within the Kremlin. ➡️ Businesses are now forced to fund their own air defense units, with costs reaching tens of millions of dollars. 🧨 Ukrainian drones are becoming less vulnerable to Russian electronic warfare systems.
